eip_8037_state_creation_costs.highlight: Target: 120 GB/year worst-case state growth, 150M block limit; new account 7x, new slot 5x, contract deploy 8x increase
Two spec bugs identified: check code hash == 0 (not code existence), and refund bytecode on delegation removal via zero-address authorization
EIP-8038 and EIP-2780 to be included in glamsterdam-devnet-4 with placeholder numbers; constant updates follow once benchmarks stabilize

eip_2780_intrinsic_gas.highlight: Current formula yields ~23K gas for ETH transfer to existing EOA—exceeding 21K baseline; benchmarks needed before finalizing values
Stefan flagged risk: avoid shipping 2780 only to plug 8037 gap; prefer correct version over rushed one

eip_8038_state_access_repricing.highlight: SSTORE formula refactored: cold/warm access and state-creation costs now separated; all SSTORE scenarios covered in new table

debug_api_for_state_gas.highlight: No consensus on debug endpoint approach; options are new debug RPC method vs. adding state gas summary to tracer output; changing receipts rejected by multiple teams

testing_progress.highlight: Besu down to 3 failing tests on 7.10 release; Hive quick tests running; Kurtosis testing starting same day
eth/70 and eth/71 now required for bal-devnet-7
ETH transfer benchmark will use YochumNet (135K unique contracts); sender accounts must be cold (forced disk read)—fix pending

action_items.action: Bring state gas debug API design (RPC vs. tracer) to ACDT next week
Update EIP-8037: remove self-destruct refunds, fix code-hash check, add delegation-removal refund
Fix ETH transfer benchmark so sender accounts are cold (force disk reads); fast-track state optimization tooling

decisions.decision: EIP-8037 spec: check code hash == 0 (not code existence); refund bytecode on zero-address authorization removal
No changes to receipts for state gas data; standardize via tracing instead
Include EIP-8038 and EIP-2780 in next Glamsterdam devnet with current placeholder numbers

targets.target: glamsterdam-devnet-4 — next Glamsterdam devnet; timing TBD
bal-devnet-7 — planned as last BAL-only devnet
